WARNING: These diagnostic steps are for A2337 (820-02016) MacBook Air 13" M1 ONLY. Direct 20V input bypasses CD3217 PD negotiation. Use current-limited supply. PMU naming: SERA=Master (U8100), SIMETRA=Slave (U7700). Test points are BOARD-SPECIFIC; do NOT use A2681 (M2) component designators.

scenarios.meaning: Board draws almost nothing at 20V. Charger circuit is not loading. Either CD3217 is dead, charger IC is not accepting input, or PPBUS_AON has an open circuit.

steps.if_missing: Overcurrent (>3A): Short circuit on input or PPBUS_AON. Disconnect immediately. Check diode mode on PPVBUS caps. Check for short on PPBUS_AON bulk caps. Check charger IC U5200. Use thermal imaging at low voltage (5V, current limited).

steps.measure: Probe PPBUS_AON bulk capacitor near U5200 output
steps.if_missing: Charger IC (U5200) not passing 20V through. Check CHGR_AUX_OK (page 24). Check for open MOSFETs in charger path. Check BMU connector J5100 SYS_DETECT (page 22).

steps.measure: Probe PP3V8_AON output capacitor near U5700
steps.if_missing: U5700 SVR AON fault. Check PPVAON_PWR_EN. Check PPVAON_FAULT_L. Check for short on PP3V8_AON_VDDMAIN (common fault on M1 boards). Thermal imaging on PP3V8_AON distribution. If shorted: systematically lift caps to isolate.

steps.measure: Probe PP3V3_AON (UC100 output), PP1V8_AON (UC120 output), SERA LDO9 (~1.8V), SIMETRA LDO9 (~1.8V)
steps.expected: PP3V3_AON=3.3V, PP1V8_AON=1.8V, SERA LDO9=~1.8V, SIMETRA LDO9=~1.8V
steps.if_missing: AON LDO or PMU LDO fault. Check specific missing rail's source IC. Thermal imaging on faulty rail. If UC100/UC120 output missing: check input PP3V8_AON_VODMAN.

steps.measure: Probe UC300 and UC710 output capacitors
steps.if_missing: Standalone VR enable or IC fault. Check P5VS2_PWR_EN and P3V3S2_PWR_EN. If enable present: VR IC fault or output short. Diode mode on output caps.

steps.measure: Check SERA BUCK0 output (PCPU DVFS), SERA BUCK11 output (ECPU DVFS), PMU_ACTIVE_READY signal. SoC power pages 11-15.
steps.expected: Multiple voltage domains present at SoC pins
steps.if_missing: SoC fault, SPI ROM fault, or NAND fault if all power present but no boot.

steps.measure: Look for Apple logo on display or external monitor
steps.if_missing: Check display connector (page 67). Check UP700/UP705/UP715 sequencer (page 68). Check DP600 (BEN) backlight boost (page 69). Try external display via USB-C.
